summaries.23896: The root cause is that using a `UniqueKey` instead of a `GlobalKey` on a `TextField` causes Flutter to lose focus state when the widget is reparented during a rebuild. Comments confirm the bug still reproduces on Flutter 1.21 dev, 3.10, and 3.12 stable on Android, with one video showing the keyboard repeatedly dismissing and re-showing on each character typed. A commenter confirms that switching to `GlobalKey` eliminates the bug, and that keeping a custom `FocusNode` alive across builds does not fix it. Currently stalled with no PR or assigned fix.
summaries.104082: Comments clarify that the issue affects both HTML and CanvasKit renderers on web, not just HTML. The root cause identified by comments is that the web engine creates a new `<input>` element in the shadow DOM each time a `TextField` loses focus, discarding the browser's native undo history. A proposed fix from the engine team (@yjbanov) is to cache `<input>` elements and reuse them rather than recreating them. Status is stalled with no linked PR.
summaries.119849: Comments identify the regression as introduced by flutter/flutter#109702, which removed the embedded `FocusScope` from `Navigator`, causing `WidgetsApp`/`MediaQuery` rebuilds to request focus and dismiss the keyboard on TextFields outside `MaterialApp`. The fix commit is a45727d81360b278cc51c5a9ae429529038cf069, which was present on master but not yet cherry-picked to stable at time of discussion. @gspencergoog provided a workaround of wrapping the app in a `FocusScope`. Users on Flutter 3.10 and 3.16 still report the issue, and some report remaining stalled on Flutter 3.3 to avoid it; no dedicated cherry-pick confirmation appears in the thread.
summaries.123101: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on macOS in addition to the original report, triggered simply by resizing the window height smaller while the TextField has a multiline hint. The issue is confirmed on both stable (3.7.7) and master. A Flutter Inspector analysis shows incorrect positioning for both hint text and input text. No root cause, PR, or fix is identified in the comments; status is stalled pending triage.
summaries.138464: Comments pinpoint the crash to `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm`'s `text(in:)` returning an invalid range when iOS predictive suggestions propose a shorter word than the currently typed text, combined with a double-tap to select. The crash occurs on iOS 17+ physical devices (confirmed on iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 11 iOS 17.1.2, iPhone 15 iOS 17.2.1) but was not reproducible on simulators. A commenter notes the crashing assertion in `text(in:)` was introduced by engine PR #16496 to fix an a11y bug, and the correct fix is to return `nil` for ranges containing `NSNotFound` instead of throwing. Workarounds reported include `autocorrect: false` combined with `keyboardType: TextInputType.name`. One commenter notes the crashing assertion is removed in Flutter 3.30.0, and another confirms they can no longer reproduce on Flutter 3.32.7.
summaries.98720: Comments narrow the root cause to older Samsung Keyboard versions (below 5.x, i.e., Android 10 and older) emitting a `ShiftRight` key-down event with non-zero `metaState` when the user switches to the symbols pane, but never emitting the corresponding key-up, leaving Flutter thinking Shift is held and causing all subsequent taps to extend the selection instead of placing the cursor. @gnprice provided detailed key-event logs confirming the synthesized shift event, and @LongCatIsLooong and @dkwingsmt identified that a forced `metaState` synchronization before text events could fix it. A user-level workaround of disabling Samsung Keyboard swipe/cursor controls or switching to 'No swipe gestures' resolves it for some; a Dart-side workaround of injecting a synthetic shift-up event also works. The issue is assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no recent status updates and no PR; a late comment links a similar reproduction using Gboard's shift-to-capitalize feature, suggesting the bug is broader than Samsung-only.
